docs.title: Welcome to frd2vtu
docs.text: A Python tool to convert CalculiX .frd files written using *element output and *node output to VTK .vtu files.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
frd2vtu converts CalculiX binary .frd files into VTK .vtu files, enabling visualization in tools like ParaView. It supports a variety of element types and can process multiple files in parallel using multiprocessing.

For more details, see the Installation, Usage, Examples, and API Reference.

docs.title: frd2vtu.frd2vtu(frd_files, parallel=True)
docs.text:
Convert one or more .frd files to .vtu format.

  • Args:
  • *frd_files (str): Paths to .frd files to convert.

  • parallel (bool): Use parallel processing (default: True).

  • Returns:

  • None

docs.title: frd2vtu.frdbin2vtu(file_path)
docs.text:
Convert a single binary .frd file to .vtu format.

  • Args:

  • file_path (str): Path to the input .frd file.

  • Returns:

  • pyvista.UnstructuredGrid if successful, None otherwise.

docs.title: frd2vtu.prepare_inp_for_binary(inp_files)
docs.text:
Prepare CalculiX input files for binary output.

  • Args:

  • inp_files (List[str]): Paths to .inp files to modify.

  • Returns:

  • None

docs.title: frd2vtu.basic_plot.basic_plots(*vtu)
docs.text:
Create simple plots for the given .vtu files.

  • Args:

  • *vtu: Variable number of .vtu file paths.

  • Returns:

  • None (saves PNG files).

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Here are some example outputs from test cases in the CalculiX test directory:

  • anipla2:

  • beamf:

  • beamp:

These .vtu files were generated using frd2vtu and visualized with frd2vtu_plot.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python >= 3.10
  • pyvista >= 0.44.2
  • pandas >= 2.2.0
  • numpy >= 2.2.6
  • rich-click
  • treeparse

These dependencies are automatically installed with the package.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: The frd2vtu tool provides a command-line interface to convert .frd files to .vtu format and prepare .inp files.

docs.title: Multiple Files
docs.text:
Convert multiple files at once (processed in parallel by default):

frd2vtu convert file1.frd file2.frd file3.frd

docs.title: Output Directory
docs.text:
Write .vtu files to a specific directory:

frd2vtu convert input.frd --output-dir ./results

docs.title: Prepare Input File for Binary Output
docs.text:
Modify a CalculiX .inp file to request binary output:

frd2vtu iprep input.inp

Optionally specify an output directory:

frd2vtu iprep input.inp --output-dir ./prepared

docs.title: Plotting
docs.text:
Generate plots from .vtu files (one PNG per file, saved alongside the VTU):

frd2vtu_plot output.vtu

Multiple files in parallel:

frd2vtu_plot file1.vtu file2.vtu

Disable parallel processing:

frd2vtu_plot file1.vtu file2.vtu --no-parallel

For more details, see the API Reference.
